# Verfix — Full Reference
> Browser verification runtime for AI-generated web apps
> Version: 0.1.4
> License: Apache 2.0
> Docs: https://verfix.dev/docs
> Source: https://github.com/verfix-dev/verfix

---

## What is Verfix?

Verfix is a local-first browser verification runtime for AI coding agents. It gives agents
a deterministic environment to verify that UI changes actually work in a real browser.

Key properties:
- Runs locally via Docker — no cloud required
- Playwright-powered browser execution
- Structured JSON output designed for agent consumption
- Exit code 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) for CI integration
- Execution timeline for human observability

Architecture:
CLI → API (Go/Fiber) → Redis queue → Playwright Workers → PostgreSQL → CLI output

---

## CLI Reference

### verfix init
Interactive setup wizard.
- Checks Docker is installed and running
- Pulls ghcr.io/verfix-dev/verfix-server:latest if missing
- Prompts: base URL, mode, flows to scaffold, AI API key
- Writes verfix.config.json
- Generates AGENTS.md
Options: --force (-f) — overwrite without prompting

### verfix start
Starts the runtime container.
Pulls image if missing. Waits for health check.
Prints API (http://localhost:3611) and Dashboard (http://localhost:3610) URLs.

### verfix stop
Stops and removes the running container. Data preserved in Docker volumes.

### verfix status [executionId]
Without executionId: shows container, API, and dashboard health.
With executionId: shows execution status, passed/failed, duration.
Options: --output pretty|json (default: pretty)

### verfix logs
Tails the runtime container logs.
Options: --tail <n> (default: 50)

### verfix update
Pulls latest image and restarts the container.

### verfix doctor
Runs 7 diagnostic checks. Exits with count of failed checks.
Checks: Docker installed, Docker daemon, container running, API healthy,
dashboard reachable, verfix.config.json exists, AGENTS.md exists.

### verfix flows
Lists all flows defined in verfix.config.json.
Options: --config <file>, --output pretty|json

### verfix run
Runs a verification job.
Options:
  --url <url>         Target URL
  --task <task>       Task description
  --config <file>     Config file (default: ./verfix.config.json)
  --flow <id>         Flow ID to run
  --mode <mode>       strict | assisted | smoke | exploratory
  --output <format>   pretty | json (default: json)
  --dashboard <url>   Dashboard base URL for timeline links
  --timeout <ms>      Timeout in ms (default: 15000)
  --retries <n>       Retries on failure (default: 2)

Exit codes: 0 = passed, 1 = failed

### verfix list
Lists recent verification executions.

---

## Output Contract

verfix run --output json returns:

{
  "passed": boolean,
  "failures": [
    {
      "type": "selector_not_found" | "selector_not_visible" | "text_mismatch" |
               "url_mismatch" | "console_error" | "network_failure" |
               "timeout" | "assertion_failed",
      "selector": string | undefined,
      "detail": string | undefined,
      "fix_hint": string
    }
  ],
  "timeline_url": "http://localhost:3610/?executionId=exec_xxx",
  "exit_code": 0 | 1,
  "execution_id": "exec_xxx",
  "raw": { /* full ExecutionResult */ }
}

---

## Failure Taxonomy

selector_not_found
  - Meaning: CSS/testId selector matched zero elements in DOM
  - Fix: Add data-testid attribute to the element; add wait_for_selector step

selector_not_visible
  - Meaning: Element exists but is hidden (display:none, visibility:hidden, zero size)
  - Fix: Check conditional rendering; ensure trigger step runs before assertion

text_mismatch
  - Meaning: Expected text was not found on the page
  - Fix: Update assertion value; add wait step for async data to load

url_mismatch
  - Meaning: Current URL does not contain the expected substring
  - Fix: Check routing configuration; verify prior flow steps succeeded

console_error
  - Meaning: One or more console.error() calls detected during flow
  - Fix: Fix the JS error in your application; check raw.console_logs for text

network_failure
  - Meaning: A required network request returned non-2xx status
  - Fix: Ensure backend is running; check raw.network_requests for URL/status

timeout
  - Meaning: An operation exceeded configured timeout (default: 15000ms)
  - Fix: Increase timeout in config or step; fix slow page load

assertion_failed
  - Meaning: Generic fallback — no more specific type applies
  - Fix: Read detail field; open timeline_url for full replay

---

## Config Schema (verfix.config.json)

Top-level:
  baseUrl: string (required) — app base URL
  mode: "strict" | "assisted" | "smoke" | "exploratory" (default: "strict")
  flows: Flow[] (required)
  assertions: AssertionDefinition[] (optional) — global assertions
  selectors: Record<string, string> (optional) — named selector map
  metadata: object (optional)
  timeout: number (optional, default: 15000ms)
  retries: number (optional, default: 2)

Flow:
  id: string (required)
  name: string (optional)
  mode: string (optional) — overrides global mode
  steps: FlowStep[] (required)
  assertions: AssertionDefinition[] (optional)

FlowStep actions:
  navigate  — { action: "navigate", url: string }
  click     — { action: "click", selector?: string, testId?: string, text?: string }
  type      — { action: "type", selector?: string, testId?: string, value: string }
  wait_for_selector — { action: "wait_for_selector", selector?: string, testId?: string }

AssertionDefinition types:
  page_loaded           — no extra fields required
  selector_visible      — selector: string
  text_visible          — value: string
  url_contains          — value: string
  title_contains        — value: string
  no_console_errors     — no extra fields required
  network_request_success — value: string (URL pattern)

---

## Execution Modes

strict:
  Selector strategy: data-testid → CSS only
  AI usage: None
  Reliability: 5/5
  Best for: CI/CD, regression testing

assisted:
  Selector strategy: data-testid → semantic → AI fallback
  AI usage: Recovery only (when selector fails)
  Recovery chain: exact → semantic (aria-label, role, text) → AI (LLM DOM analysis)
  Reliability: 4/5
  Best for: Active development, evolving UIs

exploratory:
  Selector strategy: AI-planned
  AI usage: Core driver
  Reliability: 2/5
  Best for: Discovery, ad-hoc QA, unknown flows

---

## Agent Retry Loop

Pseudocode (TypeScript):

async function verifyAndFix(flowId: string, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < maxRetries; attempt++) {
    const { stdout } = await exec(`verfix run --flow ${flowId} --output json`);
    const result = JSON.parse(stdout);

    if (result.passed) return; // verified

    for (const failure of result.failures) {
      // failure.type tells you what broke
      // failure.fix_hint tells you exactly how to fix it
      await applyFix(failure);
    }

    if (attempt === maxRetries - 1) {
      // Surface to human
      throw new Error(`Open: ${result.timeline_url}`);
    }
  }
}

---

## Self-Hosting

docker run -d \
  --name verfix \
  -p 3610:3610 \
  -p 3611:3611 \
  -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... \
  -v verfix-data:/var/lib/postgresql/15/main \
  -v verfix-artifacts:/app/workers/artifacts \
  --add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway \
  ghcr.io/verfix-dev/verfix-server:latest

Health check: GET http://localhost:3611/api/v1/health → { "status": "healthy" }

Environment variables:
  OPENAI_API_KEY    — Required for assisted/exploratory modes
  OPENAI_MODEL      — Default: gpt-5.5
  MAX_CONCURRENCY   — Default: 3

Volumes:
  verfix-data       — PostgreSQL data (execution history)
  verfix-artifacts  — Screenshots, traces, HAR files

Ports:
  3610 — Dashboard (execution timeline UI)
  3611 — API (Go/Fiber REST)

---

## AGENTS.md

verfix init generates AGENTS.md in your project root.
This file tells coding agents (Claude, Cursor, Codex) how to use Verfix in this project.
It includes: base URL, flow IDs, retry loop instructions, failure taxonomy, output contract.
Agents that read AGENTS.md will automatically use Verfix after UI changes.
